courseleaf.title: Construction Engineering & Management, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext:
California's construction industry continues to experience high growth as it continues to accommodate an increasing population and as it faces renewal of much of the infrastructure built by previous generations. At the same time, the construction industry faces challenges of scarce materials, a need for decreased environmental impact in terms of the construction process itself and operation of the completed project, and a more complex operational, regulatory and social context in which it must do its work.

The purpose of this minor is to better prepare professional civil and environmental engineers to thrive in this challenging industry and to improve the efficiency of delivery and sustainability of the civil infrastructure in the state through their future efforts.

It is expected that engineering students will enroll in this minor program, including Civil, Mechanical, Chemical, and Materials Science majors. Students from other colleges can also complete this minor, but it is recommended that they review the requirements with one of the advisors before enrolling. Students who are interested in the construction of infrastructure in their discipline can make use of the skills that will be learned through the completion of this minor.

All courses must be taken for a letter grade. A grade of C- or better is required for all courses used to satisfy minor requirements, with an overall GPA of 2.000 or better. All prerequisites for courses must have been taken for a letter grade; no grade lower than a C- will be accepted in any prerequisite course. 

The online Minor Declaration form is available via the Online Advising Student Information System (OASIS). For more information, contact the undergraduate advisors in the Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering.

Transcript notation must be requested no later than the quarter preceding graduation, and will appear as a minor in Construction Engineering & Management.

Course Overlap

Please refer to your college’s policies regarding overlap rules.

Minor Advisors

C. Bronner, S. Miller, J. Bolander, S. Nassiri
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Course List
Code Title Units
Civil & Environmental Engineering8
ECI 137
Construction Principles & Project Management
ECI 153
Deterministic Optimization & Design
Sustainability Requirement; choose one:4
ECI 123
Urban Systems & Sustainability
ECI 133
Structure & Properties of Civil Engineering Materials 1
Engineering Requirement; choose two:8
ECI 133
Structure & Properties of Civil Engineering Materials 1
ECI 178
Pavement Engineering & Design
ECI 179
Pavement Management, Evaluation, & Rehabilitation
ECI 181
Construction Cost Estimation & Analysis
ECI 182
Buildings: Assemblage & Construction Quality Management
ECI 183
Heavy Civil Construction
Economics & Law Requirement; choose two:8
ARE 018
Business Law 2
ARE 112
Fundamentals of Organization Management
ARE 118
Tax Accounting
ARE 155
Operations Research & Management Science
ARE 157
Analysis for Operations & Production Management
ECN 134
Financial Economics
or ECN 134Y
 Financial Economics
ECN 162
International Economic Relations
or ECN 162Y
 International Economic Relations
ESP 161
Environmental Law
MGT 011A
Elementary Financial Accounting 2
Total Units28
1

ECI 133​ may be used only once; may be used for the Sustainability Requirement or the Engineering Requirement.

2

Students may take only one lower-division course for the minor.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: The Construction Engineering & Management minor prepares professional civil & environmental engineers to thrive in the challenging construction industry and to improve the efficiency of delivery & sustainability of the civil infrastructure through their future efforts.
